items.description: A local editable install exposes a `parakeet` CLI from `pyproject.toml`, and `transcriber.py` remains a compatible entry path into the packaged dictation flow.
steps: Create the package/build scaffold and install the project into the repo virtual environment in editable mode
Invoke the packaged `parakeet` entry point and confirm it starts the CLI instead of failing on import or packaging errors
Run `transcriber.py` and confirm it still reaches the dictation entry path rather than diverging from the packaged CLI

items.description: Shared typed models and application error codes exist for audio, diagnostics, benchmark, config, transcription, and VAD engine boundaries.
steps: Create shared types and error-code modules under `src/parakeet/` for the command contracts defined in the source SPEC
Update the CLI-facing code to import and use those shared types or error codes instead of ad hoc inline structures
Run automated checks and confirm the shared contracts load cleanly without breaking the packaged CLI

items.description: Configuration resolution obeys deterministic precedence: CLI flags override environment variables, environment variables override config file values, and config file values override built-in defaults.
steps: Implement config loading for the milestone-1 fields described in the source SPEC
Exercise the configuration path with automated tests that cover all precedence layers
Verify the resulting runtime configuration matches the expected winning values for each precedence case

items.description: `parakeet devices --json` returns the stable device object schema from the source SPEC and handles the zero-device case with an empty array.
steps: Run the packaged `parakeet devices --json` command in a controlled test scenario with one or more input devices available or mocked
Confirm the JSON output contains `schema_version` and a `devices` array of stable device objects sorted by device id
Run the zero-device scenario and confirm the command still returns a valid JSON object with `devices: []`

items.description: `parakeet doctor` classifies WSL, Pulse, audio-device, clipboard, and CUDA readiness without loading or downloading the model in default mode.
steps: Run the packaged `parakeet doctor --json` command under mocked or controlled backend conditions
Confirm the output matches the documented top-level schema, issue mapping, and exit-code behavior for ok, warn, and fail states
Verify default doctor mode does not trigger model load or network download while still reporting actionable readiness information

items.description: `parakeet doctor --check-model-cache` reports local Parakeet cache and import readiness without performing a model download or full model load.
steps: Run `parakeet doctor --check-model-cache --json` in a scenario with local cache presence and confirm the model readiness fields are populated
Run the same command in a scenario where local cache or imports are unavailable and confirm the documented issue codes are surfaced deterministically
Verify both paths avoid network download and full model initialization

items.description: Transcript normalization and fixture sidecar loading implement normalized exact match exactly as defined by the source SPEC.
steps: Add the normalization utility and fixture sidecar loader used by benchmark and correctness checks
Run automated tests proving punctuation-only and case-only differences still match while lexical differences do not
Verify a `.expected.txt` sidecar is discovered and normalized exactly as the source SPEC requires

items.description: `parakeet benchmark --json` measures prerecorded WAV fixtures deterministically, reports the documented timing fields, and supports expected-transcript checking.
steps: Run `parakeet benchmark --fixture tests/fixtures/short_16k.wav --runs 5 --json` on a local prerecorded fixture
Confirm the JSON output contains `load_ms`, `run_ms`, aggregate timing fields, transcript fields, and arithmetic consistent with the source SPEC
Run the expected-text path and confirm `normalized_match` is computed from the sidecar transcript contract

items.description: The repo contains a CI-safe automated test suite and fixture assets that verify config precedence, doctor classification, devices JSON, benchmark JSON, and normalization without microphone or network requirements.
steps: Add or update fixture files and pytest coverage for the milestone-1 contracts
Run the automated test suite in a way that does not require a live microphone or network download
Confirm the tests provide deterministic pass or fail evidence for the command contracts defined in the source SPEC

items.description: Transcript output adapters support text and JSON stdout output, optional file output, and non-fatal clipboard failures consistent with the source SPEC.
steps: Run dictation or transcription output flows through text and JSON output modes using automated or controlled inputs
Confirm `--output-file` writes the same rendered content to disk without suppressing stdout behavior unexpectedly
Simulate clipboard failure and verify the command still succeeds while surfacing only the allowed warning behavior

items.description: WebRTC-VAD auto-stop ends recording only after voiced audio plus the configured silence window, while manual start and manual stop remain available at all times.
steps: Start a dictation session with `--vad` enabled using controlled audio input or mocks that include voiced and non-voiced segments
Confirm auto-stop triggers only after cumulative voiced duration reaches the configured threshold and the required silence window elapses
Run the no-voiced-frame scenario and confirm the session remains manual-stop only rather than stopping automatically

items.description: README usage, installation, diagnostics, and benchmark guidance reflect the packaged CLI, milestone-1 commands, and current migration path from `transcriber.py`.
steps: Update README examples so a user can install, run, diagnose, and benchmark the packaged CLI from a fresh repo checkout
Confirm the documented commands match the actual packaged command surface implemented in the codebase
Verify the migration notes explain the continued role of `transcriber.py` if the compatibility wrapper still exists
